---
title: "Morgan Mathison"
id: hero-heading
image: circleprofile.png
about:
  template: jolla
  links: 
    - icon: star
      text: Curriculum Vitae
      href: Mathison_CV.pdf
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/morganmathison/
    - icon: briefcase
      text: KU Natural History Museum Page
      href: https://biodiversity.ku.edu/people/morgan-mathison-paleobotany
    - icon: pencil
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0003-7452-2637
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/morgangm
---

Hello! I am Morgan Mathison. I am a Graduate Student at the [University of Kansas](https://biodiversity.ku.edu/people/morgan-mathison-paleobotany){style="color: pink"}, In the department of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology. I am in the Paleobotany Division of the Natural History Museum! Using the fossil record of conifers, my research seeks to investigate the evolution, diversification, and phylogenetic relationships of spermatophytes to understand how seed plant morphology has changed throughout time. 🌱🔬

Prior to my current position, I was a postbaccalaureate researcher at the University of Kansas, where I described and characterized a Cornalean fossil from the Late Cretaceous of Antarctica.

I earned my BS in Plant Science at the University of Missouri in May of 2025, where I also completed a minor in Biology and a certificate in Retail Floral Management. As an undergraduate, I was involved in research and assisted in a USDA lab, where I worked on utilizing drone imaging to high-throughput phenotype maize plants and comprehend photosynthetic productivity.

This website serves as a living Curriculum Vitae, containing my past and current projects, as well as presentations and achievements!
